prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: One thing you might be wrong about is the assumption that people can always tell when you're nervous or insecure. Often, our internal feelings of anxiety or self-doubt are much more apparent to us than they are to others. People are often too preoccupied with their own thoughts and concerns to notice every little detail about someone else. So, even if you feel very self-conscious, you might come across as confident and composed to those around you.
